title: Flag-state API gap: src/ lacks __gmpfr_flags analog for *_p predicate ports
description:
Shadow trial 2026-05-24 surfaced: the 4 flag-state predicate ports (mpfr_underflow_p, mpfr_overflow_p, mpfr_nanflag_p, mpfr_divby0_p) plus the broader exception family (mpfr_inexflag_p, mpfr_erangeflag_p, mpfr_set_underflow, mpfr_set_overflow, mpfr_clear_flags, mpfr_flags_save, mpfr_flags_set, etc.) all depend on a global flag register that doesn't exist in src/.

C analog: mpfr/src/exceptions.c L37-L40 declares 'mpfr_flags_t __gmpfr_flags', a static unsigned int holding 6 bits (UNDERFLOW=1, OVERFLOW=2, NAN=4, INEXACT=8, ERANGE=16, DIVBY0=32). All exception-flag ops read/write this register.

Two options for closing the gap:
(a) Add src/internal/mpfr/flags.ts (~30 LOC) exporting:
    - const MPFR_FLAGS_{UNDERFLOW,OVERFLOW,NAN,INEXACT,ERANGE,DIVBY0} = bigint constants
    - getFlags() -> bigint
    - setFlags(bits: bigint) -> void   (OR-combine with existing)
    - clearFlags(bits: bigint = ALL) -> void
    Then the predicates become 3-line wrappers: 
(b) Park the predicates pending an ADR on global-state APIs in the otherwise immutable-value-typed library.

Recommendation: (a). The flag register is the ONLY documented global state in MPFR; an isolated module localizes it and keeps the rest of src/ pure. ~10 dependent functions in the exception family across the next mega-batch.

Surfaced by: opus prep dispatch for shadow trial; spec.json files for the 4 predicates document the gap in divergence_from_c with both options enumerated.
close_reason: Landed src/internal/mpfr/flags.ts (~50 LOC with docs + 11-test suite); ported all 4 predicate functions (mpfr_underflow_p, mpfr_overflow_p, mpfr_nanflag_p, mpfr_divby0_p), each at composite=1.0 and mutate.py gate_passed=True. State.db: 120 done, 4 blocked.

title: mutators.ts: op-swap must not mutate function declarations
description:
## Context
Step 5 mutator calibration (docs/reports/009-mutator-calibration.md, Pattern M2).

op-swap's regex \\bmpfr_add\\( matches the function's OWN declaration in src/ops/add.ts L489:

    export function mpfr_add(

The mutator rewrites this to:

    export function mpfr_sub(

which collides with imports / breaks the named-export contract. Module init fails.

## Affected fixtures (N=6 calibration)
- mpfr_add op-swap -> module_init_failed

## Fix
Exclude matches preceded by 'function ' or 'export function '. The regex should fire only on CALL sites, not declarations. Add a negative-lookbehind or pre-mask declarations.

Same root cause analysis applies to all 4 op-pair targets (mpfr_add, mpfr_sub, mpfr_mul, mpfr_div): each would self-mutate its own declaration.

## Risk
LOW. mutate.py handles init-failed as discard; gate decisions unaffected. Performance + cleanliness fix.

## Reference
- docs/reports/009-mutator-calibration.md Pattern M2
- eval/driver/mutators.ts opSwap function
- /tmp/calib_n6.json fixture mpfr_add
close_reason: Both mutator regex bugs fixed: op-swap uses (?<!function ) look-behind to skip function declarations; ternary-negate scans forward from the matched closing brace to skip destructuring lhs (next non-whitespace = is '=' without '==' or '=>'). 4 new tests added; 26/26 mutator tests pass. Real-source smoke: op-swap on src/ops/add.ts now applies to 2 call sites (was 3 incl. its own decl); ternary-negate on src/ops/div_2si.ts now applies to 3 object-literal returns (skipping destructuring). +12 LOC mutators.ts, +18 LOC mutators.test.ts.

title: mutators.ts: ternary-negate must not mutate destructuring patterns
description:
## Context
Step 5 mutator calibration (docs/reports/009-mutator-calibration.md, Pattern M1).

ternary-negate's regex /([{,])(\s*)ternary:\s*([^,};\n]+?)(\s*[,};])/g matches inside destructuring patterns like:

    const { mant, exp, ternary: tr } = roundMantissa(...);

and rewrites it to:

    const { mant, exp, ternary: -(tr) } = roundMantissa(...);

This is invalid TS syntax (destructuring rename targets must be identifiers, not expressions). Bun rejects the module at parse time.

## Affected fixtures (N=6 calibration)
- mpfr_div_2si line 137
- mpfr_div_2ui line 199

Both registered module_init_failed for ternary-negate. mutate.py correctly detects and discards via _detect_module_init_failed; gate decisions are unaffected. But the wasted grader run is ~1 sec per occurrence, and for 500+ ports in Production this adds up.

## Fix
Exclude lines preceded by 'const|let|var ' (or any destructuring context). One option: in the regex, require the preceding `{` to NOT be part of a destructuring lhs. Cheaper option: skip if the line contains '= ' after the closing brace (indicating an assignment target).

## Risk
LOW. mutate.py already handles init-failed mutations gracefully. This is a performance + cleanliness fix, not a correctness fix.

## Reference
- docs/reports/009-mutator-calibration.md Pattern M1
- eval/driver/mutators.ts ternaryNegate function
- /tmp/calib_n6.json fixtures mpfr_div_2si, mpfr_div_2ui
close_reason: Both mutator regex bugs fixed: op-swap uses (?<!function ) look-behind to skip function declarations; ternary-negate scans forward from the matched closing brace to skip destructuring lhs (next non-whitespace = is '=' without '==' or '=>'). 4 new tests added; 26/26 mutator tests pass. Real-source smoke: op-swap on src/ops/add.ts now applies to 2 call sites (was 3 incl. its own decl); ternary-negate on src/ops/div_2si.ts now applies to 3 object-literal returns (skipping destructuring). +12 LOC mutators.ts, +18 LOC mutators.test.ts.

title: Analysis: calibrate.py results across per-class samples
description:
Step 5 of pre-trial validation. Use eval/driver/calibrate.py (just shipped) and the now-complete golden corpus to characterize how well the mutate.py menu performs across function classes.

Deliverables:
1. docs/reports/009-mutator-calibration.md covering:
   - Aggregate stats: gate pass rate, clean-kill rate per class
   - Per-class patterns: which mutations work for substrate vs arithmetic vs misc vs conversion
   - Per-mutation patterns: universal vs class-specific effectiveness
   - Outliers: functions with 0 clean kills despite multiple applicable mutations
   - Recommendation: menu strengthening targets if any class has fundamental gaps

2. Follow-up bds for actionable findings: e.g. if arithmetic shows <30% gate pass rate with current menu, file P2 bd for menu extension targeting arithmetic ports

This is READ-ONLY analysis. Do not modify mutators.ts, mutate.py, calibrate.py, or any port.

Sample size: run with --sample-per-class 4 (16 ports total ~20-40s wall) for primary data, plus --sample-per-class 6 for one cross-check.

The smoke run at --sample-per-class 1 already surfaced striking patterns: substrate 3/3 clean kills (composite 0.18); arithmetic + conversion 0/3 (composite 1.0). Larger samples need to confirm or refute this.
close_reason: Analysis complete at N=6 per class (24 fixtures). Gate pass rate 79.2% across all 4 classes (arithmetic 5/6, conversion 4/6, misc 5/6, substrate 5/6) — the N=1 smoke pattern was sample noise, gate is functional across classes. Per-mutation: shift-direction-swap dominates (87.5% clean kills); rnd-swap dead weight (0%); comparison-swap weak (5%); others 13-50%. Filed 5 follow-up bds: 2 P2 mutator regex bugs (ternary-negate corrupts destructuring; op-swap corrupts function decls — both caught by module_init_failed but waste runtime), 2 P3 improvements (gate eligibility for trivial-body ports; comparison-swap multi-site), 1 P4 (branch-replacement rnd-swap variant). Recommendation: keep menu as-is for trial; address class gaps via menu extension not threshold relaxation. Report: docs/reports/009-mutator-calibration.md.

title: calibrate.py: drive mutate.py across N functions per class, emit aggregate report
description:
Step 4 of pre-trial validation plan. Build eval/driver/calibrate.py to run mutate.mutation_prove across an explicit list of (function, port, golden) tuples and produce a per-class summary.

Public API:
- calibrate(fixtures: list[CalibrationFixture]) -> list[mutate.ProveResult]
- CalibrationFixture(function: str, port_path: Path, golden_path: Path, class_: str)
- discover_fixtures(state_db_path, eval_functions_dir, repo_root, *, sample_per_class=2) -> list[CalibrationFixture]
  Reads state.db, picks sample_per_class done ports from each class
- format_summary(results) -> str  -- markdown tables

CLI:
- python3 eval/driver/calibrate.py [--functions F1 F2 ...] [--class CLASS] [--sample-per-class N] [--summary | --json]
- Default: sample 2 ports per class (4 classes -> 8 ports total)
- --functions overrides sampling: explicit list of function names; ports/goldens resolved automatically

Output sections in --summary:
1. Per-function table: function | class | applicable mutations | clean kills | gate passed | notes
2. Per-class aggregate: class | n_functions | total mutations applied | clean kills | mean composite
3. Per-mutation aggregate: mutation | times applicable | clean kills | mean composite when applied

Implementation:
1. Read state.db, filter status='done' rows
2. Group by class, sample N per class with stable seeding
3. For each: resolve port_path (src/ops/<short>.ts or src/internal/...) and golden_path (eval/functions/<full_name>/golden.jsonl); skip if either is missing
4. Call mutate.mutation_prove for each (sequential, not parallel — keeps logs readable and avoids /tmp races)
5. Aggregate + format

Acceptance:
- TDD with pytest cases: mock mutate.mutation_prove for the aggregation logic; real smoke test against 1 fixture (mpfr_add_d)
- Sample selection is deterministic (fixed seed) so re-runs match
- Handles missing ports/goldens gracefully (warn to stderr, skip from sample)

- ASCII only

title: gen_spec: extend _KNOWN_TYPES and _classify_return scalar mappings
description: gen_spec emits `TODO: <type> <name>` for params and `TODO: <type>` for returns whenever a C scalar/handle type isn't in its known list. This is honest but incomplete; ~39/80 param diffs and ~18/55 returns diffs contain a TODO that is mechanically fixable. Missing param types: `long int`, `unsigned long int`, `signed long`, `mpz_ptr`, `mpq_ptr`, `mpf_ptr`, `mpfr_limb_ptr`, `mpfr_exp_t`, `mpz_srcptr`/`mpq_srcptr`/`mpf_srcptr` (const variants already known but non-const not). Missing return-type mappings: `double` -> `number`, `long`/`unsigned long`/`int` non-Result return -> `bigint` for prec_t/exp_t scalars, `void` -> `MPFR` when the first dropped pointer is the only output. See docs/reports/008-gen-spec-delta.md "P7" + "P8". Low complexity; would clean up many spec stubs that otherwise need manual repair. Blocks step 6 only insofar as TODO stubs propagate into porter prompts.
close_reason: All 3 P2 gen_spec extraction bugs fixed in one subagent dispatch. Extraction errors 8 -> 0; all 26 previously-affected functions (5 parens-name + 3 static hard-error + 18 static silent-wrong) now extract correctly. Field disagreement drops: returns 52.9% -> 24.1%; params 76.9% -> 67.9%. Tests: 8 existing pass + 11 new pass (5 standalone + 6 parametrized). Fixes in gen_spec.py: (1) parens-name regex extension '\\b<name>\\s*\\)?\\s*\\('; (2) removed static-skip + added _TYPE_TAIL validation rejecting heads ending in control-flow tokens; (3) extended _KNOWN_TYPES (long int, mpz_ptr family, mpfr_exp_t etc) and _classify_return scalar mapping (double->number, prec_t/exp_t/long->bigint, void-with-rop->MPFR). Subagent also caught and fixed an out-of-scope-but-necessary _return_type bug (was returning 'static int' instead of 'int' for newly-accepted static decls).

title: gen_spec: stop unconditionally skipping static decls when callgraph lists them
description: gen_spec._find_signature drops any candidate head that starts with `static`, which causes hard errors on 3 functions whose only definition IS static (mpfr_add1sp1, mpfr_div2_approx, mpfr_mul_1) AND silently wrong output on 18 more (mpfr_add1sp{1n,2,2n,3}, mpfr_sub1sp{1n,2,2n,3}, mpfr_mul_{1n,2}, mpfr_sqr_{1,1n,2}, mpfr_div_{1,1n}, mpfr_overflow, mpfr_powerof2_raw2, mpfr_mpn_cmp_aux) where the scan continues past the static decl and lands on a call site inside a sibling dispatcher. The call-site extraction produces c_signature like `if (p == GMP_NUMB_BITS) return mpfr_add1sp1n (a, b, c, rnd_mode)` and TODO-littered params. Fix proposal: (a) do not skip static heads -- callgraph.json is the authority on which functions to port; (b) additionally validate that the matched head ends in a return-type token (reject heads ending in `return`, `?`, `)`, `}`). See docs/reports/008-gen-spec-delta.md "P2" + "P3". Affects ~21% of the corpus and is the highest-impact extraction bug. Blocks step 6.

title: gen_spec: handle parenthesized-name macro-override decls (mpfr_inf_p style)
description: gen_spec._find_signature errors out on 5 functions whose C decl wraps the function name in parens to override a same-named macro: `int (mpfr_inf_p) (mpfr_srcptr x) { ... }`. The regex `\b<name>\s*\(` matches the inner name but the look-back includes `int (` and the leading paren breaks the decl/call disambiguation, returning None. Affected: mpfr_inf_p, mpfr_nan_p, mpfr_regular_p, mpfr_sgn, mpfr_zero_p. Fix: detect and strip a leading `(` before the name during head extraction, OR teach the regex to optionally consume a wrapping paren-pair around the name. See docs/reports/008-gen-spec-delta.md "P1". Verify by re-running `python3 eval/driver/validate_specs.py --function mpfr_inf_p` -- should produce a non-error result. Blocks step 6 (ralph-loop integration of gen_spec).

title: Analysis: gen_spec.py delta across 119 curated spec.json files
description:
Step 3 of pre-trial validation. Use eval/driver/validate_specs.py (just shipped) to characterize how gen_spec.extract_spec output diverges from the curated spec.json corpus.

Deliverables:
1. docs/reports/008-gen-spec-delta.md — analysis report covering:
   - Aggregate stats: how many functions match exactly, how many have ≥1 structural diff, how many have extraction errors
   - Per-field disagreement rates (class, params, returns, c_signature, prec_unit)
   - Common patterns: what kinds of diffs recur? (e.g. class budget-tier vs semantic family; param name semantic vs literal-C)
   - Outliers: functions where gen_spec fails entirely (extraction errors) or differs across all 5 fields
   - Recommendation: should gen_spec be modified before step 6 (ralph.py integration), should curated specs be normalized, or should both be accepted as different but compatible?
2. Follow-up bds filed for any actionable findings (e.g. gen_spec systematic bug on a class of functions)

This step is READ-ONLY. Do not modify gen_spec.py, validate_specs.py, or any spec.json. The report's job is to characterize, not fix.

Acceptance:
- Report file exists with the sections above
- Bds filed (or explicitly noted as 'no action needed') for each pattern surfaced
- Pure analysis; no code changes to anything except creating the new report file
close_reason: Analysis complete. Findings: 0/104 functions match gen_spec on all 5 structural fields. 23% (26/112) have hard extraction bugs — 8 hard errors + 18 silent-wrong (call-site harvested as decl). Filed 6 follow-up bds: 3 P2 gen_spec extraction fixes (parens-name, static-skip, call-site detection + KNOWN_TYPES extension), 1 P3 ADR for documented divergences, 2 P4 curated-drift cleanup. title: validate_specs.py: structural diff of gen_spec output vs curated spec.json across all functions
description:
Step 2 of pre-trial validation. Build eval/driver/validate_specs.py to run gen_spec.extract_spec against every function we have a curated spec.json for, and emit a structural diff report.

Public API:
- validate_spec(c_source_path, function, curated_spec_path) -> SpecValidationResult
- validate_all_specs(eval_functions_dir, mpfr_src_dir, callgraph_path) -> list[SpecValidationResult]
- main() CLI

Dataclasses (frozen=True):
- SpecFieldDiff(field: str, gen_spec_value, curated_value, matches: bool)
- SpecValidationResult(function: str, c_source: Path, curated_spec: Path, diffs: list[SpecFieldDiff], extraction_error: str | None)

Fields to diff structurally (not prose):
- class (str equality)
- signature.params (list equality, order matters)
- signature.returns (str equality)
- c_signature (str equality with whitespace normalization on both sides)
- prec_unit (str equality)

Fields to SKIP (intentionally differ): function (always input), doc, divergence_from_c, refs.

CLI behavior:
- python3 eval/driver/validate_specs.py [--json | --summary | --function FN]
- --summary (default): pretty table per-function, plus aggregate stats (N functions checked, N matched, N with diffs, N with extraction errors)
- --json: machine-readable per-function results
- --function FN: only check one function (useful for debugging)

Mapping function -> C source: read eval/driver/callgraph.json's 'functions' object, lookup defined_in field, prepend mpfr/src/.

Acceptance:
- TDD with pytest cases for the dataclasses + diff logic + a smoke test against 2-3 real fixtures (mpfr_add_d, mpfr_cmp, mpfr_init2)
- Handles gracefully: extract_spec raises, callgraph missing entry, C source file missing
- 100-150 LOC for validate_specs.py + 60-90 LOC tests
- pytest passes
close_reason: validate_specs.py landed: 167 LOC tool + 108 LOC tests (7 pytest cases, all pass). CLI works: --function add_d --summary shows class/c_signature diffs; --function cmp --summary shows class/params/c_signature/prec_unit diffs. Surfaced new findings on first 2 fixtures: (1) curated 'class' field tracks runtime budget tier (misc=1000ms) not semantic family, so gen_spec's filename heuristic systematically disagrees; (2) curated params use semantic names (a/b) where gen_spec uses literal C decl names (b/c); (3) some curated specs are missing prec_unit. Analysis of full 119-function corpus is step 3.

title: Diverse import-shape regression tests for _rewrite_relative_imports
description:
Step 1 of pre-trial validation plan. Currently _rewrite_relative_imports in eval/driver/mutate.py is tested implicitly by the calibration tests (which only exercise simple 'import { ... } from "../core.ts"' shapes). Before relying on it across the full codebase of 115 ports, harden it with explicit regression tests covering the import shapes that actually appear in the codebase.

Shapes to cover:
1. Multi-line imports: 'import {\n  mpfr_add,\n  mpfr_mul,\n} from "../ops/index.ts";'
2. Re-exports: 'export { mpfr_add } from "../ops/add.ts";'
3. Type-only imports: 'import type { MPFR } from "../core.ts";'
4. Mixed type imports: 'import { type MPFR, RoundingMode } from "../core.ts";'
5. Side-effect imports (must NOT be rewritten if no from clause): 'import "./polyfill.ts";' (defensive — these would also need rewriting but might not be in mpfr-ts)
6. Bare-package imports (must NOT be touched): 'import { x } from "some-package";'
7. Relative non-core: 'from "../internal/mpn/add_n.ts"'

Acceptance:
- 5-8 new pytest cases added to eval/driver/tests/test_mutate.py
- Each case uses a helper that calls mutate._rewrite_relative_imports directly with a synthetic source string
- TDD: red first (any case where the function fails to handle the shape), then green if a fix is needed; if all current shapes already work, document that and add the tests as regression coverage
- LOC delta: +30 to +50 LOC in test_mutate.py (currently 145; cap 200)
- All existing 6 tests in test_mutate.py continue to pass
close_reason: Added 8 import-shape regression tests covering: multi-line imports, re-exports, type-only imports, mixed type imports, sibling-relative paths, cross-tree relatives, double-quoted variants, bare-package non-rewrite. All pass without any mutate.py change — existing regex (?<=from )(['\"])(\.\.?/[^'\"]+)\1 is already robust because every shape's path follows the literal 'from ' keyword. +39 LOC delta to test_mutate.py (145 -> 184). All 14 tests pass (8 new + 6 existing including 2 slow calibration runs).

title: Strengthen mutator menu: current 4-mutation menu clean-kills only 1/6 across calibration fixtures
description:
The mutate.py calibration tests (mpfr_add_d, mpfr_sqr_2) revealed that the current 4-mutation menu in eval/driver/mutators.ts is structurally too weak to replace opus's hand-picked broken-port deliverable.

Observed calibration after import-rewrite fix (2026-05-24):

mpfr_add_d (3 applicable mutations):
  op-swap        composite=0.0000  clean kill ✓
  rnd-swap       composite=1.0000  NOT killed — golden RNDN/RNDZ cases produce identical results often
  ternary-negate composite=1.0000  NOT killed — most golden cases have ternary=0; negating 0 = 0

mpfr_sqr_2 (3 applicable mutations):
  rnd-swap       composite=0.8930  weak kill (below 0.95 but not <0.55)
  ternary-negate composite=1.0000  NOT killed — same exact-result issue
  sign-flip      composite=1.0000  NOT killed — sign-flip only touches NaN/inf/zero special-case branches

1/6 clean kills total. The original risk-mitigation target was 4/6 (set in mutate.py prompt). Current menu cannot serve as a drop-in replacement for opus's hand-designed perturbation.

PROPOSED MENU EXTENSIONS:
1. constant-bump: find <int>n bigint literals in non-import contexts (e.g., 53n, 63n, 192n) and bump by ±1. Catches off-by-one prec/shift bugs.
2. comparison-swap: '<' ↔ '<=' and '>' ↔ '>=' in conditional contexts. Catches boundary errors.
3. shift-direction-swap: '>>' ↔ '<<' (BigInt shifts particularly). Catches direction-confusion bugs.
4. arith-binop-swap: '+' ↔ '-' in non-call positions (e.g., 'a + b' but not 'mpfr_add(...)'). Catches sign errors in inline arithmetic.

DESIGN CONSIDERATIONS:
- constant-bump is the most likely to land clean kills broadly (touches the exponent/sticky-bit math that ternary-negate misses)
- comparison-swap requires careful AST-context detection (regex on conditions only, not assignments)
- shift-direction-swap is high-value for substrate ports (mpn_* functions are shift-heavy)
- arith-binop-swap risks matching '+' in string concatenation; needs constraint to numeric contexts

ACCEPTANCE: re-running mutate.py calibration tests on mpfr_add_d + mpfr_sqr_2 + mpfr_cmp + mpfr_div_1 (4 fixtures) should show ≥3 clean kills total (vs current 1 across 2 fixtures).

LOC budget: ~80 LOC added to mutators.ts (currently 101) + ~50 LOC of new tests. Total ~150 LOC delta.

This is the follow-up promised in the step 3 commit message and worklog 008. Not blocking the broader automation effort — mutate.py is structurally sound and lands gate_passed=True for ports where op-swap applies. Strengthens once this lands.
close_reason: Menu extended with 3 new mutations: bigint-bump (single-target bump of first bigint literal >=2), comparison-swap (swap <,<=,>,>= in first runtime occurrence with generics-guard), shift-direction-swap (bidirectional >>/<<). Plus maskComments helper (caught during calibration: bigint-bump was matching JSDoc literals). Real calibration: 3 clean kills across mpfr_add_d + mpfr_sqr_2 (vs prior 1, target was 3). shift-direction-swap landed composite=0.0 on sqr_2; bigint-bump landed 0.528 on sqr_2. comparison-swap weak on both fixtures (hits prec-validation guards not exercised in normal-path tests) — worth a P4 follow-up but not blocking.

title: mutate.py: Python orchestrator that runs mutators + grader, validates calibration
description:
Build eval/driver/mutate.py: Python orchestrator that drives mutators.ts + runner.ts to mutation-prove a port.

Public API:
- mutation_prove(function_name, port_path, golden_path, tmp_dir=None) -> ProveResult

ProveResult dataclass:
- function_name: str
- mutations: list[MutationResult]  -- per applicable mutation
- gate_passed: bool                -- at least 1 mutation scored <= 0.95 (CLAUDE.md PIL.3 threshold)
- clean_kills: int                 -- count of mutations that scored <= 0.55 (worklog 006 #6 standard)

MutationResult dataclass:
- name: str
- composite: float | None    -- None if mutator returned applied=false
- below_threshold: bool      -- composite <= 0.95
- clean_kill: bool           -- composite <= 0.55

CLI:
- python3 eval/driver/mutate.py --function <fn> --port <path> --golden <path> [--tmp-dir <path>]
  Prints a summary table and exits 0 if gate_passed, 1 otherwise.

Implementation:
1. subprocess: 'bun eval/driver/mutators.ts list --input <port>' -> list of applicable mutations
2. For each: produce /tmp/mutant_<fn>_<mut>.ts via mutators.ts apply
3. subprocess: 'bun eval/harness/runner.ts --function <fn> --port <mutant> --golden <golden> --output /tmp/grade_<fn>_<mut>.json'
4. Parse grade.json, extract composite_correctness
5. Aggregate + return

Calibration target (the actual gate this is meant to enforce):
- For mpfr_add_d (3 applicable mutations): ALL applied mutations must score <= 0.95; >=2 must score <= 0.55
- For mpfr_sqr_2 (3 applicable mutations): same

Tests live in eval/driver/tests/test_mutate.py. Calibration tests against the two real fixtures above run as part of pytest (slower but mandatory).

Acceptance:
- TDD: red first, then green
- pytest passes against both fixtures
- 150-200 LOC total (orchestrator + tests)
- subprocess timeouts of 30s per grader call (prevent infinite loops from a bad mutant)
- Cleanup: mutate.py removes /tmp/mutant_*.ts and /tmp/grade_*.json on exit

Risks the subagent must guard against:
- Bad mutant infinite-loops in the grader -- subprocess timeout
- grade.json malformed -- catch JSONDecodeError, treat as composite=None
- Mutator exits with code 3 (not applicable) when listApplicableMutations said it applies -- treat as test failure (calibration drift)
- mpfr-ts-6s9: bunfig.toml preload=[] is cwd-sensitive -- invoke bun from repo root explicitly
close_reason: mutate.py landed: 242 LOC orchestrator + 140 LOC tests (6 pytest cases, all pass). Import-rewrite + module_init_failed detection both functional. Calibration findings: current menu lands 1/6 clean kills across mpfr_add_d + mpfr_sqr_2; menu-strengthening filed as follow-up bd.

title: mutators.ts: regex-based AST mutator engine for mutation-proving
description:
Build eval/driver/mutators.ts: a Bun-native regex-based mutator that applies one of several deterministic perturbations to a TS port file.

Public API:
- applyMutation(source: string, mutation: MutationName): { mutated: string; applied: boolean; description: string }
- listApplicableMutations(source: string): MutationName[]
- CLI: 'bun eval/driver/mutators.ts apply --input <path> --output <path> --mutation <name>'
- CLI: 'bun eval/driver/mutators.ts list --input <path>' -- prints applicable mutations

Mutation menu (4 to start):
1. op-swap: swap ALL occurrences of mpfr_add(/mpfr_sub( pairs and mpfr_mul(/mpfr_div( pairs (whichever appears first in the file)
2. rnd-swap: swap ALL occurrences of 'RNDN'/'RNDZ' or 'RNDU'/'RNDD' (string-literal form only)
3. ternary-negate: find 'ternary: <ident-or-literal>' in return positions and negate the value
4. sign-flip: swap 'sign: 1' with 'sign: -1' (and the reverse if present)

Each mutation returns applied=false (and unchanged source) if no matching pattern is present.

Style: regex-based, matches eval/harness/ast_check.ts convention. No AST library deps.

Acceptance:
- TDD: red test first, then green
- Tests cover each mutation applied + each mutation no-op case
- Tests assert applied mutants differ from source AND parse cleanly (round-trip via Bun.Transpiler or similar)
- Cyrillic-safe: do not introduce non-ASCII chars
- Comments/strings: brief check that the regex doesn't accidentally match inside line comments or string literals containing the trigger token
- 150-200 LOC total (mutator + tests)
- 'bun test eval/driver/mutators.test.ts' passes
close_reason: mutators.ts landed: 101 LOC + 99 LOC tests (12 bun:test cases, all pass via bun test from eval/driver/). 4 mutations (op-swap, rnd-swap, ternary-negate, sign-flip) all confirmed via real-source smoke test on add_d.ts and sqr_2.ts. Round-trip transpile via Bun.Transpiler verifies syntactic validity of every applied mutant. Subagent caught + fixed a real bug mid-implementation: initial ternary-negate regex matched 'const ternary: Ternary = ...;' declarations and broke sqr_2 transpile; tightened to require leading { or , for object-literal context.

title: gen_spec.py: extract structural spec fields from C source
description:
Build eval/driver/gen_spec.py with extract_spec(c_source_path, function_name, class_hint=None) -> dict.

Outputs a partial spec.json dict with these structural fields populated:
- function (str): the function name
- class (str): one of {substrate, arithmetic, transcendental, misc} — inferred from C source path (mpn -> substrate; add*.c/sub*.c/mul*.c/div*.c/sqr*.c/sqrt*.c -> arithmetic; exp*.c/log*.c/sin*.c/cos*.c/tan*.c/asin*.c/acos*.c/atan*.c/sinh*.c/cosh*.c/tanh*.c -> transcendental; else misc). Class hint overrides.
- signature.params (list[str]): TS param names derived from C param names, dropping the output slot (mpfr_ptr first arg)
- signature.returns (str): 'Result' if last C param is mpfr_rnd_t and first is mpfr_ptr; 'number' if C returns int and no mpfr_ptr; etc.
- c_signature (str): the exact C signature line, normalized to single line
- prec_unit (str): always 'bits'
- doc (str): 'TODO: opus fills this in'
- divergence_from_c (str): 'TODO: opus fills this in'
- refs (list[str]): templated minimum: mpfr/src/<fn>.c, src/core.ts, CLAUDE.md hallucination callouts

Acceptance:
- TDD: red test first (shows failing), then green (shows passing)
- Tests cover: mpfr_add_d (simple wrapper), mpfr_sqr_2 (single-limb fast path), mpfr_cmp (no rop, returns int), mpfr_get_str (string output, complex sig), mpfr_init2 (substrate-style init)
- Unknown C types emit 'TODO: <type>' not a guess
- 150-200 LOC total including tests
- pytest eval/driver/tests/test_gen_spec.py passes
close_reason: gen_spec.py landed: 152 LOC module + 111 LOC tests (8 pytest cases, all pass). Real-source smoke-tested against mpfr/src/add_d.c, cmp.c, init2.c, get_str.c — handles multi-line decls, static-skipping, unknown-type TODOs correctly.

title: Run `bd export -o .beads/issues.jsonl` before every git commit
description: The mpfr-ts-NEW1/2r8/akk/etc engine issues from the previous-session HANDOFF were never persisted to git because no `bd export` ran before the commits. Local dolt had them; JSONL did not; new device pulled and lost them. The HANDOFF.md gotcha #2 documents this trap. Either: pre-commit hook that does the export, or a wrapper around bd-mutating commands. Until then, all bd changes need a manual export step before commit.

title: Logging API ADR for MPFR_LOG_* facility
description: Needed to unblock mpfr_log_begin and the rest of mpfr/src/logging.c family. C body is static __attribute__((constructor)) inside #ifdef MPFR_USE_LOGGING; reads 7+ env vars and writes log file. TS port surface needs ADR: env vars vs callback registry vs no-op. Surfaced at worklog 021 PREP triage.

title: state.db port_path field records vanished /tmp/eval_*/port.ts tmpdirs
description:
## Context
Surfaced during HANDOFF.md Priority 1 work (bd mpfr-ts-9di, worklog 016, Phase 1 baseline).

For some functions, sqlite3 eval/state.db 'SELECT port_path FROM runs WHERE fn_name=X ORDER BY started_at DESC LIMIT 1' returns paths like /tmp/eval_mpfr_set_inf/port.ts and /tmp/eval_mpfr_get_d1/port.ts. The tmpdirs no longer exist on disk by the next session.

The workaround in worklog 016 Phase 1 was to substitute src/ops/<base>.ts for the 2 affected cases (mpfr_set_inf, mpfr_get_d1). This worked because the published port is the persistent artifact -- but it required a manual translation step that future re-grade loops will keep tripping over.

## What needs to be done
Either:
a) Recording: when a run promotes a ported file into src/ops/, update runs.port_path to the published src/ops/<base>.ts path. (preferred: state.db then points at the durable artifact.)
b) Recovery: on re-grade, if port_path doesn't exist on disk, fall back to src/ops/<base>.ts. (band-aid: keeps stale tmpdir history.)

Option (a) is cleaner. Likely lives in eval/driver/ralph.py or wherever the promote-to-src step is.

## Risk
LOW. Only affects re-grade scripts and ad-hoc mutation-prove invocations, not the live ralph loop. Workaround is trivial. But every future agent that wants to re-grade an old function will hit it.

title: Port mpfr_asprintf: needs TS-native format API ADR
description: C is a varargs printf-style formatter. TS has no native varargs. Need API decision: %Rf-style format vs toString-style vs template-literal tagged helpers. See eval/functions/mpfr_asprintf/spec.json.

title: Port mpfr_strtofr: needs TS-native string-IO API ADR
description: C is a 750-LOC parser with base/locale/special-string handling. TS surface needs an API decision (toString-style vs strtofr-style). Multi-week port requiring upstream design. See eval/functions/mpfr_strtofr/spec.json.

title: Port mpfr_add_z: needs mpz/bigint integration ADR
description: C signature uses mpz_srcptr. Natural TS surface is mpfr_add_z(x: MPFR, z: bigint, prec, rnd). Need ADR on: (1) whether _z variants are exposed at all vs composing via mpfr_set_from_bigint + mpfr_add; (2) bigint-to-mantissa coercion. See eval/functions/mpfr_add_z/spec.json.
close_reason: ADR 0003 shipped (bigint as mpz_t analogue). 5 _z functions ported at composite=1.0: mpfr_set_z_2exp, mpfr_get_z_2exp, mpfr_add_z, mpfr_sub_z, mpfr_mul_z. Mutate gate: 4 killed + 1 low-confidence-pass; zero survived. Chunk 2 complete.

title: mutators.ts: comparison-swap should enumerate all sites, not first-match
description:
## Context
Step 5 mutator calibration (docs/reports/009-mutator-calibration.md, Patterns P3, M3).

comparison-swap uses single-match regex semantics: it mutates the FIRST applicable comparison in the source, not all of them. When a port has a precondition check before the algorithmic comparison, the precondition wins:

    if (i < n) { ... }              // <-- mutator picks this
    ...
    if (a > b) return 1;            // <-- algorithmic comparison missed
    if (a < b) return -1;

## Affected fixtures (N=6 calibration)
- mpn_cmp: comparison-swap mutated `i < n` (line 133, precondition); composite=1.0. The intended target `a > b` / `a < b` (line 167) was untouched.
- mpfr_mpn_cmpzero: composite=0.77; gate passes but doesn't clean-kill.

Aggregate clean-kill rate for comparison-swap across 24 fixtures at N=6 is 1/20 = 5%. This is the worst-performing mutation outside rnd-swap (which has its own bd, P4).

## Fix
Iterate all applicable comparison sites, emit N mutants (one per site), and gate-pass on min(composite_i) <= 0.95. Cost: N x grader_time_per_mutation. For most ports N <= 5, so the budget impact is modest. The mutator can be capped at N=3 to bound cost.

## Risk
MEDIUM. Increases grader time. But comparison-swap is currently near-dead-weight; a higher cost with materially better coverage is a good trade.

## Reference
- docs/reports/009-mutator-calibration.md Patterns P3, M3
- eval/driver/mutators.ts comparisonSwap function
- /tmp/calib_n6.json fixtures mpn_cmp, mpfr_mpn_cmpzero

title: mutate.py: 'low-confidence-pass' carve-out for thin-surface ports
description:
## Context
Step 5 mutator calibration (docs/reports/009-mutator-calibration.md, Patterns P1, P2).

The mutation-prove gate currently returns gate_passed=False for ports too trivial to mutate productively. Examples from N=6 calibration:

- mpfr_swap: 0 applicable mutations (body is just `return { a: b, b: a }`). Gate FAILS.
- mpfr_set_inf: 2 applicable mutations (op-swap, sign-flip), both at composite=1.0 because the function reduces to a 3-way switch on sign and constant posInf/negInf returns. Gate FAILS.
- mpfr_get_d1: 1 applicable mutation (rnd-swap), composite=1.0. Body is `return mpfr_get_d(x, _defaultRoundingMode);` -- a pure delegation. Gate FAILS.

These ports are CORRECT BY CONSTRUCTION but the gate flags them. False negatives waste reviewer attention.

## Fix options (pick one)
a) Gate passes when zero mutations apply (the port has no algorithmic surface).
b) Gate consults a "complexity floor" derived from applicable-mutation count: if <2 mutations apply, the gate is exempt and the port is flagged for human review of the test surface, not for re-porting.
c) spec.json may carry `mutation_prove_exempt: true` for the small set of ports (~5) where this applies.

Option (a) is the simplest. Option (b) provides better signal. Option (c) is most explicit but requires spec edits.

## Risk
LOW. Affects ~5 ports out of ~600. The false-negative gate failures cost reviewer time but don't propagate wrong code.

## Reference
- docs/reports/009-mutator-calibration.md Patterns P1, P2
- eval/driver/mutate.py _aggregate_gate, ProveResult
- /tmp/calib_n6.json fixtures mpfr_swap, mpfr_set_inf, mpfr_get_d1, mpfr_get_d_2exp
notes:
CLOSED 2026-05-25 via worklog 016 (docs/worklog/016-mutate-carve-out.md).

Shipped: option (b) complexity-floor carve-out — added 4th gate_status value 'low-confidence-pass'. Predicate (final, refined from HANDOFF spec via TDD):
- >=1 mutation applied
- ZERO below_threshold
- count(applied AND not init_failed) <= 2
- ALL such mutations have composite > 0.99 (strict)

Refactor: _aggregate_gate now delegates to _gate_status (single source of truth, cannot drift).

Diff: 33 net LOC in eval/driver/mutate.py + 34 LOC of new tests in eval/driver/tests/test_mutate.py. Full pytest suite: 119 -> 123 passing, no regressions.

Re-grade of the 7 known cases:
- Flipped to low-confidence-pass (5): mpfr_sqrt1, mpfr_set_inf, mpfr_get_d1, mpn_copyi, mpn_copyd
- Stayed survived (2): mpn_zero (composite 0.9783 < 0.99 floor — real perturbation), mpfr_sub1sp (3 applied > 2 count — genuine algorithmic surface)

The 2 that stayed survived are correctly flagged: they need stronger mutators/goldens, not a more lenient gate.

The HANDOFF's literal predicate ("all >= 0.95") would have broken pre-existing tests; the TDD RED-first discipline caught it. See worklog 016 'TDD spec-hole catch' section.
close_reason: Shipped option (b) complexity-floor carve-out. See worklog 016 + closing notes.

title: ADR: document curated divergences from gen_spec (class as budget tier, ident names, idiomatic-TS returns)
description: Three structural fields disagree between gen_spec and curated specs in ways that are NOT bugs but ARE undocumented decisions that re-litigate every time an agent looks at a spec: (1) curated `class` is the runtime budget tier (arithmetic=50/200ms, misc=1000ms, substrate=internal) not the semantic family that gen_spec's filename heuristic produces; ~60% disagreement is expected. (2) curated parameter names and c_signature identifier names mirror the public mpfr.h prototype (rop, op1, op2, x, rnd) not the C definition's body-level idents (a, b, c, rnd_mode) that gen_spec lifts. (3) curated returns use idiomatic TS types: `boolean` for `_p` predicates, `bigint` for prec_t/exp_t/long/unsigned long, object wrappers for harness-couplig (e.g. mpfr_print_rnd_mode returns `{name: string}`). Action: write docs/adr/NNNN-gen-spec-curated-divergences.md spelling these out so the step 6 spec-merge step knows which fields to prefer-curated-over-generated. Recommend ADR before step 6 lands so the merge policy isn't reinvented per-PR. See docs/reports/008-gen-spec-delta.md "P4" + "P5" + "P6" + "P8".
close_reason: ADR 0001 landed at docs/adr/0001-spec-merge-policy.md. Documents per-field precedence for gen_spec vs curator: c_signature/prec_unit-default go to gen_spec; class/params/returns/doc/divergence_from_c go to curator; refs[0] gen_spec + curator extends. This unblocks step 6 (ralph.py wiring) — the prompt template can now follow this policy mechanically. Estimated opus savings per function: ~5-8 lines boilerplate + ~30-60s thinking. Correctness benefit: c_signature is always machine-accurate; refs[0] always canonical.

title: callgraph.py misses mpn_* substrate fns (sourced from GMP)
description: callgraph.py walks mpfr/src/*.c only; mpn_* helpers live in the GMP source tree, not MPFR. They're referenced by deps lists but never appear as candidate fns. Means --next can never schedule a missing mpn_* port. Fix: also walk gmp/mpn/generic/*.c (or stub mpn_* entries). Old issue mpfr-ts-9li.

title: Enforce Rule 7 tag minimums at grade time
description: CLAUDE.md Rule 7 says goldens must have happy>=20, edge>=30, adversarial>=10, fuzz>=50, mined>=5. Currently no automatic enforcement — agents can ship low-coverage goldens and they'll pass. Grader should reject (or warn) when class counts fall short. Old issue mpfr-ts-7em.

title: runner n_throw conflates exceptions with value mismatches
description: The runner reports n_throw for any non-pass case, including value mismatches that did NOT throw. Hit this session investigating mpn_cmp_aux failures: report said n_throw=11 but cases were returning wrong VALUES, not throwing. Misleading for diagnosis. Should split: n_throw (actual exceptions) vs n_mismatch (value disagreement). Old issue mpfr-ts-3ka.

title: expected_throw codec for goldens with domain errors
description: Functions like mpfr_abort_prec_max are blocked because the harness has no way to encode 'this input should cause the port to throw'. Currently the only output codec emits a value. Adds: a wire-level 'expected_throw' field to golden entries that the grader matches against caught exceptions from the port. Old issue mpfr-ts-79u.

title: Add eval/golden_master/run_all.sh wrapper
description: build.sh compiles drivers; there's no scripted equivalent for running them to produce golden.jsonl. Currently the orchestrator runs per-driver: `./golden_driver > golden.jsonl` in a bash loop. A run_all.sh that iterates eval/functions/*/ would mirror build.sh and let the HANDOFF's documented `bash eval/golden_master/run_all.sh` actually work. Old issue mpfr-ts-0t3 (lost in dolt sync gap).
close_reason: run_all.sh landed (85 LOC, executable, shellcheck-clean). Iterates eval/functions/*/, invokes each compiled golden_driver with 60s timeout, redirects stdout to golden.jsonl. Idempotent (skip if exists unless --force). --filter <fn> for single-function runs. Cleans up partial goldens on failure/timeout. Sibling of build.sh; same style. Smoke-tested in 4 modes.

title: Bug averted: bd-init .gitignore '*.db' glob would have excluded eval/state.db
description: During Step 1 (mpfr-ts-odi.1) the subagent noticed bd init's default .gitignore contained '*.db' which would have silently excluded eval/state.db — violating CLAUDE.md Rule 9 (state.db is the single source of truth and MUST be checked in). Fix: replaced glob with explicit ignores and a sentinel comment. Filing as closed for the historical record so future agents debugging 'why is state.db not in git' can find this.

title: Park mpfr_init_cache under ADR 0002 -- disabled-in-C, no public caller

title: mutators.ts: add branch-replacement rnd-swap variant
description:
## Context
Step 5 mutator calibration (docs/reports/009-mutator-calibration.md, Pattern P6).

rnd-swap clean-killed 0/16 applications at N=6 with mean composite 0.984. It swaps quoted string literals ('RNDN' <-> 'RNDZ', 'RNDU' <-> 'RNDD') -- but most ports either pass `rnd` through to a delegated function unchanged (so the swap only affects unreachable string-literal-in-default branches) or operate on values where the rounding-mode difference is invisible for the golden cases (exact returns, zeros, infinities).

Golden tag distributions are healthy (5-class coverage on tested fns); the issue is the mutator surface, not the test surface.

## Proposed variant
Mutate DISPATCH sites rather than literals:

    if (rnd === 'RNDN') { ... }     // becomes
    if (rnd === 'RNDZ') { ... }     // forces wrong dispatch

This forces a wrong code path for any RNDN-passed input, which is the most common case in goldens.

## Risk
LOW. Additive: keep existing rnd-swap, add a second variant. If the new variant lands kills, great; if not, it joins rnd-swap as dead weight at ~1 sec cost.

## When to revisit
After fixes from comparison-swap multi-site (its bd) and the M1/M2 mutator bugs land. The trial run can proceed without this fix; it's a coverage extension, not a correctness blocker.

## Reference
- docs/reports/009-mutator-calibration.md Pattern P6
- eval/driver/mutators.ts rndSwap function

title: curated drift: c_signature space-before-paren and identifier renaming across corpus
description: 100% of c_signature fields disagree between gen_spec and curated specs. After whitespace normalization, 46/104 disagreements collapse to a single difference: gen_spec emits 'mpfr_add (...)' (GNU style, space before paren) following the C *definition*, while curated emits 'mpfr_add(...)' following the *prototype* in mpfr.h. The remaining 58 are deeper renames (curated uses public-API idents: rop/op1/op2; gen lifts def-body idents: a/b/c) and minor 'long int' vs 'long'. This is a known-style decision (curated mirrors mpfr.h). Action: either (a) teach gen_spec to find the header prototype when available and prefer it; (b) post-process to strip space-before-paren; (c) leave the divergence and document in the ADR (see mpfr-ts-n8y). Trivial fix #b would close the 46 cosmetic cases without affecting the 58 deeper renames. See docs/reports/008-gen-spec-delta.md "P4".

title: curated drift: prec_unit inconsistent on no-prec-param functions
description: 20/104 curated specs disagree with gen_spec's unconditional prec_unit='bits'. Of these: 16 are predicates/comparators (mpfr_cmp*, mpfr_*_p) where curated omits the field (correct -- no prec param, field is inapplicable); 3 are mpn_*_aux substrate where curated says 'bits (limb-level)'; 1 (mpfr_print_rnd_mode) says 'n/a'. The inconsistency itself ('bits (limb-level)' vs 'n/a' vs absent for similarly inapplicable cases) is mild curated drift. Action: either (a) extend the prec_unit enum to {bits, bits-limb-level, n/a, absent-by-design} and normalize all curated specs, OR (b) leave alone -- the field is purely informational. Low priority; defer until something actually depends on prec_unit having a consistent meaning. See docs/reports/008-gen-spec-delta.md "P9".

title: mine_tests.py: skip — MPFR test files use variable-fed inputs, not lift-able literals
description:
Investigated as part of the opus-prep automation effort (step 2 of 5). Conclusion: skip.

MPFR test files (e.g. mpfr/tests/tadd_d.c) overwhelmingly use variable-fed inputs at call sites: 'mpfr_add_d (y, x, d, MPFR_RNDN)' where y/x/d are constructed via earlier mpfr_set_* calls. Naive literal-arg extraction yields 0-3 usable cases per function (just the rounding modes and inline scalars).

A full dataflow miner that tracks mpfr_set_* assignments back to feeding literals is 300+ LOC, well over the original 100-150 LOC budget, and has silent-failure modes (incorrect lifts produce 'mined' cases that don't match libmpfr).

Per-function leverage is ~3 min of opus work — the smallest item in the original automation ranking. Cutting it saves ~10 hours across remaining functions, vs ~70 hours each from the mutator and driver-scaffold steps. Opus is genuinely good at the cognitive part of mining (picking representative shapes from a test file by reading prose context).

Decision: defer mining automation indefinitely. Reconsider only if a less-fragile approach surfaces (e.g. parsing mpfr's existing test-data JSON files, if they exist for any subset of functions).
close_reason: Decision recorded; not actioning.

title: state.db topo_rank diverges from callgraph topo_rank
description: Seeded rows store topo_rank from the callgraph at seed time. If callgraph.py is re-run (e.g. after pulling new mpfr/), ranks can shift but seeded rows keep old values. Picker uses state.db rank, which may now be inconsistent. Either: re-seed all rows on callgraph regen, or always read topo_rank live from callgraph.json. Old issue mpfr-ts-0dw.
